chunks.text: SM/PS ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI C.P. No. D-8963 of 2018 Faisal Mubeen Jumani & others Versus Province of Sindh and others ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE(S) OF JUDGE(S) ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- BEFORE: Justice Irfan Saadat Khan, Justice Muhammad Faisal Kamal Alam,JJ 1. For orders on office objection 2. For hearing of CMA No.24918/2020 (U/o.39) 3. For hearing of CMA No.39385/2018 (U/o.39) 4. For hearing of main case. --------- 08.10.2021 Ms. Farida Mangrio,advocate for the petitioners. Mr. Ahmed Pirzada, Advocate for the alleged contemnors No.1&2. Mr. Miran Muhammad Shah, Addl. A. G. Sindh for all the Respondents & alleged contemnors No.3&4. -.----.---.- This petition has been filed on the ground that the petitioners claim that they are entitled for doing the business of cattle and dairy farms on the land total measuring 128-00 acres situated in Deh Kharkaro, Taluka Gadap, District Malir, Karachi, however, the official respondents are creating hurdles in this regard. Thereafter, notices were issued to the respondents in respect of which comments were filed. Today, while arguing the matter, Ms. Farida Mangrio, counsel for the petitioner has categorically stated that the said land which, has a proper lease in her favour, would be used exclusively for cattle and dairy farming and would neither be used for plotting nor for residential / commercial purposes. Her this statement is taken on record and on the basis of this statement, the instant petition stands disposed of.

Farida Mangrio, counsel for the petitioner has categorically stated that the said land which, has a proper lease in her favour, would be used exclusively for cattle and dairy farming and would neither be used for plotting nor for residential / commercial purposes. Her this statement is taken on record and on the basis of this statement, the instant petition stands disposed of. However, before parting with the order, we would like to observe that the respondents would act strictly in accordance with law and if 2 they intend to take any adverse action against the petitioners an opportunity of hearing in this regard would be provided to them. Instant petition alongwith all the listed and pending applications, if any, stands disposed of as above. JUDGE JUDGE SM
